reasoning: Currently, the US economy is showing robust signs with Q3 2024 GDP growth at 2.8% and the Federal Reserve actively normalizing interest rates (which supports a 'soft landing'). Goldman Sachs places the 12-month recession probability at a historically low 15%. Furthermore, the strict resolution criteria—requiring two consecutive negative quarters strictly within the 2025 calendar year based on initial/advance estimates—significantly curtails the probability. Recessions starting too early (Q4 2024 + Q1 2025) or too late (Q4 2025 + Q1 2026) would not trigger a 'YES'. Because the criteria eliminate cross-year occurrences, it naturally reduces the probability of a qualifying recession by about 40% compared to a generic any-two-quarters metric. Combined with the strong economic momentum carrying into early 2025, the likelihood of this specific scenario playing out is very low, close to the market consensus of ~2-3%.
